behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert on specific implementation details like variable name '$blockPattern' and '$blockMatch' (test_updateSdkVersion_uses_block_scoping, test_block_scoped_version_update_logic) and parameter name '$existingPrUrl' (test_updateExistingPr_signature_updated), none of which are mentioned in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes bugs and expected fixes at a high level without naming exact variables or line numbers.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests hard-code gold variable names: test_block_scoped_version_update_logic requires '$blockPattern' or '$blockMatch'; test_updateExistingPr_signature_updated requires parameter named '$existingPrUrl'; test_cleanupTarget_removed requires both 'chmod' and 'rm -rf' strings — alternative valid fixes (e.g., using different variable names or PHP native rmdir/unlink) would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All behavioral tests are essentially file_get_contents + strpos/preg_match grep checks against source code; no test actually invokes updateSdkVersion(), pushToGit(), or updateExistingPr() to verify behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require specific string patterns to be present; a stub would not contain them.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Each of the six issues in instruction.md has at least one corresponding test.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are named descriptively and grouped with comments separating behavioral from pass-to-pass tests.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task does not produce structured data output.

no_typos.reason: No apparent typos in paths or names.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the file (required for the task) and method names but not specific lines or internal variable names.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, not a config edit.
